construct.label: Existential and identity AI anxiety anchor
construct.definition: Anxiety arising from the belief that advancing AI systems may threaten human distinctiveness, purpose, dignity, creativity, status, or self-understanding.

scenario_text: Imagine that an AI system wins several major open competitions in fiction, illustration, music composition, and product design after judges review entries without knowing whether they were made by humans or AI. The system can explain its creative choices, imitate many artistic traditions, and generate work that audiences describe as moving and original. Human artists still create work, but public debate shifts toward whether human creativity has any special status beyond personal expression.

response_scale.prompt: How anxious would this scenario make you about the special status of human creativity and creative identity?

scoring.missing_response_rule: Treat skipped responses as missing at the item level. Do not impute single-item scores for primary analyses.
exclusion_criteria: Exclude respondents who fail the survey-level attention check specified in the study protocol.
Exclude respondents who complete the item in less than one-third of the median reading time for the administration wave.
Exclude respondents who report that they could not understand the scenario after reading it.
Exclude respondents younger than the minimum eligible age for the study population.

interpretation_bands.label: low
interpretation_bands.interpretation: Respondent reports little or no anxiety about AI performance challenging the status of human creativity.

interpretation_bands.label: moderate
interpretation_bands.interpretation: Respondent reports a meaningful but not severe anxiety response to AI systems competing with human creative identity.

interpretation_bands.label: high
interpretation_bands.interpretation: Respondent reports strong anxiety that advanced AI could diminish perceived human distinctiveness, creative status, or personal meaning.

population.target_population: Adults in the general population, with planned subgroup analysis for creative professionals, students in creative fields, and respondents who report creative practice as central to identity.
population.eligible_respondents: Respondents aged 18 or older who can complete the survey language without assistance and provide informed consent.
exclusion_criteria: Current inability to provide informed consent.
Duplicate panel participation within the same measurement wave.
Self-reported inability to read the survey language.

administration_mode.instructions: Present the scenario text exactly as written, followed immediately by the response prompt and the five ordered response options.
administration_mode.randomization: Randomize item order within the cross-domain anchor pool while preserving the ordered response scale.
administration_mode.longitudinal_use: Use identical wording, response anchors, scoring, and eligibility rules across waves unless a new item version is released.

ethics_flags.data_protection_notes: Do not collect unpublished creative work, portfolio links, real competition entries, or identifiable professional reputation details for this item.
ethics_flags.irb_notes: The item presents a hypothetical public recognition scenario and may be identity-salient for creative workers. Include a debrief clarifying that the item measures response to a scenario, not creative ability.
